input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In Android 11+ (API 30+), `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated, and .NET MAUI has been moving toward using `WindowInsets`-based approaches.

In .NET 10 (which builds on newer Android APIs), the recommended approach is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` or handle keyboard insets properly.

Let me provide a concise answer based on my knowledge:


The main replacement for the deprecated `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` is `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll`, which has been the default behavior since .NET 8 and automatically adjusts content when the keyboard appears. However, for chat screens specifically, this automatic scrolling sometimes doesn't work as expected, so I need to think through what the actual solution pattern should be for that use case.
